items.title: The Juries (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order replaces the Juries Acts (Northern Ireland) 1871 to 1974. The Order revises the law relating to qualification for jury service and the empanelling, summoning and balloting of jurors. It also further assimilates the law regarding coroners' juries.

items.title: The Commissioner for Complaints (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (revoked)
items.description: This Order repeals and re-enacts with amendments the Commissioner for Complaints Act (Northern Ireland) 1969. The principal amendments make provision for—

items.title: The Ombudsman (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (revoked)
items.description: This Order repeals and re-enacts with amendments the Parliamentary Commissioner Act (Northern Ireland) 1969. The principal amendments make provision for—

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends and largely restates the law in relation to the confiscation of the proceeds of crime.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes provision with respect to the prosecution and punishment (including the punishment without conviction) of road traffic offences in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Deregulation and Contracting Out (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order contains miscellaneous provisions relating to deregulation and the contracting out of functions of Northern Ireland departments and certain office-holders. It also confers power to improve enforcement procedures and requires model provisions to be made about appeals against enforcement action, with a view to their being incorporated in legislation.

items.title: The Food Safety (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes miscellaneous amendments to the Food Safety (Northern Ireland) Order 1991. The principal amendments extend the provisions of that Order to enable the Department of Agriculture to exercise functions under that Order in relation to all foods for which it has responsibility.

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Residual Liabilities) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (repealed)
items.description: The Order imposes a duty on the Department of Health and Social Services, when certain Health and Social Services bodies cease to exist, to exercise its statutory powers as respects the transfer of property, rights and liabilities of the defunct bodies so as to secure that all of their liabilities are transferred to other Health and Social Services bodies or the Department.

items.title: The Appropriation (No. 2) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order authorises the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of Northern Ireland of a further sum for the year ending on 31st March 1997 and appropriates that sum for specified services in Northern Ireland. It also authorises the application of certain sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 1997. In addition the Order repeals certain spent enactments.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order is made for purposes corresponding to the purposes of section 1 of, and the Schedule to, the Education (Student Loans) Act 1996.

items.title: The Employment Rights (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order re-enacts, with minor amendments, the Contracts of Employment and Redundancy Payments Act (Northern Ireland) 1965 and the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Orders 1976 to 1993 in so far as they relate to employment rights.

items.title: The Explosives (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order enables Explosives regulations under section 3(1) of the Explosives Act (Northern Ireland) 1970 to make provision for controlling and regulating the manufacture, sale, acquisition, transfer, storing, transportation, handling, use or disposal of fireworks. It also enables Explosives regulations to exempt fireworks from the requirements of section l(1) and (2) of that Act and increases to sixteen the minimum age at which explosives (including fireworks) may be sold to any person.

items.title: The Industrial Tribunals (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order re-enacts with minor amendments the statutory provisions relating to the constitution, jurisdiction and procedure of industrial tribunals.

items.title: The Personal Social Services (Direct Payments) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 to enable the Department of Health and Social Services to make payments to persons in respect of their securing the provision of personal social services.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Payment to Third Parties) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of section 120 of the Housing Act 1996.

items.title: The Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: Part II of this Order makes new provision for the education of children with special educational needs. Part III of this Order contains miscellaneous amendments to the law of education. The principal amendments—

items.title: The Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order provides for the regulation of the supply of gas through pipes.

items.title: The County Courts (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order confers on county courts power to make certain orders for the discovery or preservation of property or documents which are, or may become, relevant to proceedings in that court.

items.title: The Domestic Energy Efficiency Schemes (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of section 142 of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996.

items.title: The Licensing (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the law relating to licences for the sale by retail of intoxicating liquor. The principal amendments are—

items.title: The Registration of Clubs (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: The Order consolidates with amendments the law relating to the registration of clubs. The principal amendments are—

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order—

items.title: The Rates (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 by—

items.title: The Succession (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order—

items.title: The Appropriation (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order authorises the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of Northern Ireland of a further sum for the year ending on 31st March 1996 and of sums on account for the year ending on 31st March 1997 and appropriates those sums for specified services in Northern Ireland. It also authorises the application of certain further sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 1996 and reduces certain sums already authorised to be applied as appropriations in aid.

items.title: The Business Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order repeals and re-enacts with amendments the Business Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 1964. The Order gives effect to recommendations of the Law Reform Advisory Committee for Northern Ireland (Report No. 1 on Business Tenancies, LRAC No. 2, 1994).

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
The Judicial Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Judicial Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995.

items.title: Supreme Court Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Supreme Court Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1994 (as amended) to:—

items.title: The Judgment Enforcement Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Judgment Enforcement Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1992 (as amended) to increase the majority of fees to be taken in respect of the enforcement of judgments under the Judgments Enforcement (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Magistrates' Courts Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1994 (as amended) so as to:

items.title: The County Court Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the County Court Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1994 (as amended) so as to:—

items.title: Supreme Court (Non-Contentious Probate) Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Supreme Court (Non-Contentious Probate) Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 (as amended) so as to:—

items.title: The Matrimonial Causes Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Matrimonial Causes Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 (as amended) to:

items.title: Dental Charges (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These regulations further amend the Dental Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989, (“the principal regulations”) which relate to charges for dental treatment provided and dental appliances supplied as part of health service general dental services.

items.title: Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 (“the principal regulations”) which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for the payment by the Department of travelling expenses incurred in attending hospital.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions), Statutory Maternity Pay and Statutory Sick Pay (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979, the Statutory Sick Pay (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1982, the Statutory Sick Pay (Mariners, Airmen and Persons Abroad) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1982 and the Statutory Maternity Pay (Persons Abroad and Mariners) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: Health and Safety (Medical Devices) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
The Social Security (Persons from Abroad) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1976, the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1984, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988, the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992, the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992.

items.title: Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992. The Regulations make the following principal changes—

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987. They make and further amend provisions relating to maximum eligible rent in cases where housing benefit is payable, including conferring a discretion on the Northern Ireland Housing Executive (“the Executive”) to pay a lesser sum in appropriate cases (regulations 3, 5 and 7). They also add a discretion to pay more benefit than would otherwise be payable by reason of these Regulations (regulation 10).

items.title: Charges for Drugs and Appliances (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973, (“the principal regulations”) which provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances supplied by doctors and chemists providing pharmaceutical services, and by hospitals and HSS Trusts to outpatients.

items.title: Motor Hackney Carriages (Belfast) (Amendment) By-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These By-Laws amend the Motor Hackney Carriages (Belfast) By-Laws made by the Council of the County Borough of Belfast on 4th June 1951 by—

items.title: General Dental Services (Amendment) Regulation z (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services General Dental Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General and Supply of Information) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Housing Benefit (Supply of Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988. These Regulations make and further amend provisions relating to maximum benefit in cases where housing benefit was payable, by providing for a further, extended, payment of benefit in prescribed circumstances and make provision for the effect of such payments on any further grant of benefit.

items.title: The Sperrin Lakeland Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order establishes the Sperrin Lakeland Health and Social Services Trust, an HSS trust provided for in Article 10 of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1991.

items.title: The Foyle Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order amends the Foyle Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 423) with effect from 25th March 1996.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order reduces from 30 miles per hour to 20 miles per hour the speed limit on the restricted roads specified in the Schedule (Article 2).

items.title: Health and Safety (Safety Signs and Signals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations impose requirements in relation to the provision and use of safety signs and signals and implement as respects Northern Ireland Council Directive 92/58/EEC (O.J. No. L245, 26.8.92, p. 23) on the minimum requirements for the provision of safety and/or health signs at work.

items.title: Motor Vehicle Testing (Extension) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: Article 63 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”) requires, subject to certain exceptions, that motor vehicles registered or manufactured more than 5 years ago, are not to be used on a road unless a valid vehicle test certificate has been issued by the Department of the Environment.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support, the new rate being 7·74 per cent.

items.title: The Children’s Evidence (1995 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order appoints 8th April 1996 for the coming into operation of the Children’s Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1995.

items.title: Health and Personal Social Services (Amendment) (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order brings into operation on 29th March 1996 all the remaining provisions of the Health and Personal Social Services (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1995, which amends the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (“the 1972 Order”) so far as they are not already in operation.

items.title: Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These regulations further amend the Optical Charges and Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 (“the principal regulations”) which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Health and Social Services Trusts (Originating Capital Debt) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order determines the amount of the originating capital debt provided for in Article 14(1) of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 of HSS trusts established under that Order with an operational date of 1st April 1995. It also provides also for the splitting of the originating capital debts into interest bearing loan and public dividend capital.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts Rules (Northern Ireland) 1984 (the principal Rules) and the Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1969 (the 1969 Rules) so as to reflect the amendments made to the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (the 1989 Order) by Articles 5(1) and (2) of the Children’s Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1995.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Children’s Evidence) (Notices of Transfer) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Rules provide for the procedure to be followed in magistrates' courts where a notice of transfer is given under Article 4(1) of the Children’s Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 which comes into operation on 8th April 1996.

items.title: The Guardians Ad Litem (Panel) Regulation (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the establishment of a panel of guardians ad litem (regulation 2); a complaints board and a panel committee (regulation 3); appointments to the panel (regulation 4); termination of panel membership (regulation 5); complaints about the operation of the panel and members of the panel (regulation 6); administration of the panel (regulation 7); panel committee functions (regulation 8); expenses, fees and allowances of panel members (regulation 9); monitoring the work of guardians ad litem (regulation 10); and training (regulation 11)

items.title: The Northern Ireland Disability Council Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the circumstances in which a member of the Northern Ireland Disability Council established by section 50 of, as modified by Schedule 8 to, the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 may be removed from office by the Department of Health and Social Services (regulation 2). They also make provision for the expenses incurred by the Council to be paid by the Department of Health and Social Services with the approval of the Department of Finance and Personnel (regulation 3).

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Fund-holding Practices) Amendment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Fund-holding Practices) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993, which regulate the recognition and operation of fund-holding practices.

items.title: Beef (Emergency Control) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prohibits in Northern Ireland the sale of any meat (as defined in Article 1(2)) from bovine animals slaughtered after 28th March 1996 and which at the time of slaughter were more than 2½ years of age.

items.title: Specified Bovine Material Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with amendments the Specified Bovine Offal Order (Northern Ireland) 1995. That Order implemented various Directives as specified in that Order. It provides that bovine heads (other than tongues) shall be disposed of as specified bovine material as well as the materials specified in the earlier Order.

items.title: The Specified Bovine Material (Treatment and Disposal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake with amendments the Specified Bovine Offal (Treatment and Disposal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 and the Mechanically Recovered Meat Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 which implemented various directives as specified in those Regulations. They provide that bovine heads (other than tongues) shall be disposed of as specified bovine material as well as the materials specified in the earlier Regulations.

items.title: General Ophthalmic Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These regulations further amend the General Ophthalmic Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 (“the principal regulations”) to make provision relating to ophthalmic medical practitioners and ophthalmic opticians (“contractors”) who have been suspended from the provision of general ophthalmic services by the Tribunal or whom the Tribunal has declared not fit to be engaged in any capacity in the provision of those services.

items.title: General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 (“the principal regulations”), which regulate the terms on which general medical and pharmaceutical services are provided under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (“the 1972 Order”).

items.title: Health and Personal Social Services (Disciplinary Procedures) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Health and Personal Social Services (Services Committee) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 (“the 1973 Regulations”) to provide for the investigation and determination by Health and Social Services Boards (“Boards”), of questions whether doctors, dentists, chemists and opticians providing services under Part VI of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 have failed to comply with their terms of service, and for the consideration and determination of appeals from determinations of Boards.

items.title: Goods Vehicles (Testing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Testing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 as follows—

items.title: Health and Safety (Medical Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations fix fees to be paid for work done by employment medical advisers in conducting medical examinations and surveillance under—

items.title: Motor Vehicle Testing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicle Testing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) by amending fees payable as follows—

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (Test Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 by increasing the fee payable for a driving test on all types of vehicles except invalid carriages (for which no fee is payable) as follows—

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Taxi Drivers' Licences) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Taxi Drivers' Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 by decreasing the fee for a renewal of a licence from £60 to £58·50.

items.title: Public Service Vehicles (Licence Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Public Service Vehicles Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985 by amending the fee payable—

items.title: International Transport of Goods under Cover of TIR Carnets (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the International Transport of Goods under Cover of TIR Carnets (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 by—

items.title: Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 and the Regulations which amended them as specified in the Schedule to these Regulations. The Regulations impose requirements regarding the installation and use of recording equipment (tachographs) in vehicles, and make provision for the inspection and repair of such equipmentl.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Payments in Respect of Applicants for Exemption from Wearing Seat Belts) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends Article 25 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 so as to enable the Department to make payments in respect of an applicant for exemption from wearing seat belts who is either in receipt of an income-based jobseeker’s allowance paid under the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 or is the dependant of such a person.

items.title: The Repairs Grants (Eligible Expense) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: Except where the Department approves a higher eligible expense, this Order specifies the maximum amount of eligible expense for the purposes of a repairs grant as £500 (formerly £5,500) in the case of grant applications made by owner-occupiers on or after 1st May 1996.

items.title: Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Grant Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order provides new maximum limits on the amount of grant which the Northern Ireland Housing Executive may pay as follows—

items.title: The Social Security (Reduced Rates of Class 1 Contributions) (Salary Related Contracted-out Schemes) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 1996/1054) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under section 42 of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies with effect from 6th April 1997 the contracted-out percentages to be deducted from primary and secondary Class 1 contributions in respect of members of salary related contracted-out schemes.

items.title: The Children (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order brings into operation on 19th February 1996 the following provisions of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995:—

items.title: The Social Security (Reduced Rates of Class 1 Contributions and Rebates) (Money Purchase Contracted-out Schemes) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 1996/1055) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under section 42B of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies with effect from 6th April 1997 the appropriate flat-rate percentage and the appropriate age-related percentages in respect of members of money purchase contracted-out schemes.

items.title: The Social Security (Minimum Contributions to Appropriate Personal Pension Schemes) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 1996/1056) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under section 45A of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies with effect from 6th April 1997 the appropriate age-related percentages of earnings payable as minimum contributions in respect of members of appropriate personal pension schemes.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Children’s Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (Notice of Transfer) Regulations 1996
items.description: These regulations provide for the form of notice of transfer under Article 4 of the Children’s Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1995. That is the notice which is sent to a magistrates' court before the start of committal proceedings by or on behalf of the Director of Public Prosecutions and which has the effect of transferring the case to the Crown Court. The regulations require copies of the notice to be sent to the defendant, the Crown Court and (if the defendant is in custody) the prison governor.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985 (“the 1985 Regulations”).

items.title: Land Registry (Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prescribes the fees to be taken in the Land Registry for the purposes of the Land Registration Act (Northern Ireland) 1970 and the manner in which those fees are to be paid.

items.title: Health and Safety (Miscellaneous Fees Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
Diseases of Fish (Control) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations implement in respect of Northern Ireland Council Directive 93/53/EEC (O.J. No. L175, 19.7.93, p. 23) introducing minimum Community measures for the control of certain fish diseases, to the extent that it is not implemented by existing legislation.

items.title: The Health and Social Services Trusts (Originating Capital Debt) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Health and Social Services Trusts (Originating Capital Debt) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 which determines the originating capital debt, interest bearing initial loan and public dividend capital of HSS trusts established under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 with an operational date of 1st April 1995.

items.title: One-Way Traffic (Omagh) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends the provisions of the One-Way Traffic (Omagh) Order (Northern Ireland) 1986.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order makes the roads and lengths of road in Schedule 1 restricted roads, thereby imposing a 30 miles per hour speed limit on those roads.

items.title: One-Way Traffic (Killyleagh) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order provides for a one-way traffic system in Shore Street, Killyleagh, from its junction with Catherine Street, to its junction with Seaview.

items.title: Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (S.R. 1993 No. 173) (“the principal Regulations”) and implement Commission Directive 95/3/EEC (O.J. No. L41, 23.2.95, p. 44), amending Commission Directive 90/128/EEC (O.J. No. L75, 21.3.90, p. 19, to be read with corrigendum in O.J. No. L349, 13.12.90, p. 26) relating to plastic materials and articles intended to come into contact with foodstuffs.

items.title: Fertilisers (Mammalian Meat and Bone Meal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations control, in the public interest, the composition or content of fertilisers used or intended for sale for use on agricultural land.

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 1996/1133) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under section 148(3) and (4) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, directs that the earnings factors relevant to the calculation of the additional pension in the rate of any long-term benefit or of any guaranteed minimum pension or to any other calculation required under Part III of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 for the tax years specified in the Schedule to this Order are to be increased by the percentage of their amount specified in that Schedule. The percentage for the tax year 1995-96 is 2·8 per cent. and those for earlier years have been increased so that the earnings factors for those years are revalued at the 1995-96 earnings levels.

items.title: Price Marking (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Price Marking Order (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the 1992 Order”) which implemented Council Directive 79/581/EEC (O.J. No. L158, 26.6.79, p. 19) (“the 1979 Directive”) as amended by Council Directive 88/315/EEC (O.J. No. L142, 9.6.88, p. 23) and Council Directive 88/314/EEC (O.J. No. L142, 9.6.88, p. 19) (“the 1988 Directive”) on consumer protection in the indication of the prices of foodstuffs and non-food products, respectively.

items.title: Urban Clearways (Belfast) (No. 2) Order (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Urban Clearways (Belfast) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1981 by—

items.title: Traffic Weight Restriction (Rosscor Viaduct Bridge) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prohibits vehicles exceeding 7.5 tonnes maximum gross weight from using Rosscor Viaduct Bridge, Belleek, County Fermanagh.

items.title: Community Work Programme (Miscellaneous Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order provides that, for the purposes of the statutory provisions specified in the Schedule, a person using facilities provided under the Community Work Programme shall be treated as participating in arrangements for training under section 1 of the Employment and Training Act (Northern Ireland) 1950. Any payment made to such a person in connection with his use of those facilities shall be treated in the same manner as a payment made in respect of such training.

items.title: Industrial Tribunals (Constitution and Rules of Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace Regulations prescribing rules of procedure for industrial tribunals in Northern Ireland.

items.title: Beef (Emergency Control) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: The Beef (Emergency Control) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 (as the principal Order) prohibited the sale in Northern Ireland of any meat from bovine animals slaughtered after 28th March 1996 and which at the time of slaughter were more than 2½ years of age. This Order amends the principal Order to exclude from the scope of that prohibition meat from bovine animals that were born, reared and slaughtered in countries specified in the Schedule inserted by Article 2(b).

items.title: Weights and Measures (Guernsey and Alderney) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: A number of the provisions of the Weights and Measures (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”) relating to the regulation of trade and weighing or measuring equipment used for trade do not apply in relation to goods “for despatch to a destination outside Northern Ireland and any designated country”.

items.title: Temporary Speed Limit (Sydenham By-Pass, Route A2, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prohibits in the interest of safety the driving of motor vehicles at more than 30 miles per hour on the eastbound carriageway of Sydenham By-Pass, Route A2, Belfast, from its junction with Middlepath Street, to its junction with Dee Street, and on the westbound carriageway of Sydenham By-Pass, Route A2, Belfast, from its junction with Dee Street, to a point approximately 240 metres west of that junction for a period of 4 months commencing on 10th June 1996.

items.title: Companies (Summary Financial Statement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 10th June 1996, replace the Companies (Summary Financial Statement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (S.R. 1993 No. 267) (“the 1993 Regulations”).

items.title: Plant Health (Wood and Bark) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Wood and Bark) Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 to implement as respects Northern Ireland—

items.title: The Jobseekers (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This, the second Order commencing provisions of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”), provides for the coming into operation on 3rd May 1996 of certain provisions in Schedule 2 which introduce into the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 8) references to the Order.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987. Regulation 4 creates a new maximum rent in the case of certain single claimants who are under 25 (“young individuals”), in respect of whom the Northern Ireland Housing Executive (“the Executive”) has determined a single room rent. Regulation 10(1) provides that these changes shall not apply to those young individuals to whom the savings provision in the Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 111) (providing exemption from the changes introduced by those Regulations) apply and regulation 10(2) provides that, when young individuals do not enjoy that savings protection, the changes introduced by regulations 3, 4 and 9 shall not apply to those in receipt of benefit on 6th October 1996 until they move home or their current benefit period comes to an end. Regulation 8 provides the manner in which a single room rent is to be determined.

items.title: The Bovine Animals (Enforcement of Community Purchase Scheme) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the enforcement of certain of the requirements of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 716/96, adopting exceptional support measures for the beef market in the United Kingdom. That Regulation authorises the United Kingdom to introduce a scheme for the purchase of any bovine animal aged over 30 months which does not exhibit any clinical sign of BSE and which was, during a period of at least three months prior to its sale, present on a holding located on United Kingdom territory. Various requirements are specified as to the slaughter, treatment and disposal of animals subject to that scheme.

items.title: The Slaughter-houses (Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Slaughter-houses (Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1955 to enable slaughter-houses to be used for the slaughter of animals which are not destined for human consumption, but which are slaughtered pursuant to a slaughter scheme introduced by or under Council Regulation (EEC) 805/68, as amended.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order, which contains only provision corresponding to a provision contained in an Order (S.I. 1996/599) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under section 150 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5), increases the maximum amount to be allowed in respect of child care charges relevant to the calculation of housing benefit from “£40” to “£60”.

items.title: Specified Bovine Material (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Specified Bovine Material Order (Northern Ireland) 1996. The Order makes the following principal changes—

items.title: Specified Bovine Material (Treatment and Disposal) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Specified Bovine Material (Treatment and Disposal) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996. The Regulations make the following principal changes:

items.title: Pharmaceutical Society of Northern Ireland (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pharmaceutical Society of Northern Ireland (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (S.R. 1994 No. 202) by increasing fees payable in respect of:—

items.title: The Social Security (Additional Pension) (Contributions Paid in Error) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations apply to cases where primary Class 1 contributions have been paid in error because the individual concerned was not an employed earner.

items.title: The Local Government (General Grant) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order—

items.title: County Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1981 so as to increase county court costs by 11.25%.

items.title: Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 10th June 1996 and have effect retrospectively from 1st September 1995 revoke and replace, with amendments, the Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the previous Regulations”).

items.title: Agricultural Processing and Marketing Grant Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 24th June 1996, supplement as respects Northern Ireland Council Regulation (EEC) No. 866/90 (O.J. No. L91, 6.4.90, p. 1) as amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 3669/93 (O.J. No. L338, 31.12.93, p. 26), which respectively contain measures for improving the processing and marketing conditions of agricultural products.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations replace Part III of the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 which relates to driving tests and make certain other incidental and minor amendments.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations are made by virtue of, or are consequential upon, provisions in the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”).

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Jobseeker’s Allowance Consequential Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the principal Regulations”). The amendments are consequential on the coming into operation of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”), which replaces income support for the unemployed and unemployment benefit with jobseeker’s allowance.

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order:

items.title: The Homefirst Community Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order establishes the Homefirst Community Health and Social Services Trust, an HSS trust provided for in Article 10 of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1991.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Transitional Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which are made pursuant to Article 39 of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) provide for continuity between, on the one hand, unemployment benefit and income support for those who are required to be available for and actively seeking employment and, on the other, a jobseeker’s allowance.

items.title: The Social Security (Back to Work Bonus) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: Article 28 of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) introduces the back to work bonus for persons who are or have been entitled to income support or a jobseeker’s allowance.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits (Maintenance Payments and Consequential Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations provide that where the Department of Health and Social Services (“the Department”) is collecting maintenance payments on behalf of a person who is claiming certain social security benefits, the Department may disregard those maintenance payments for the purposes of calculating the claimant’s benefit entitlement, and may then retain any such maintenance payments collected by it.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Internal Dispute Resolution Procedures) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: The Pensions (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1996 No. 91 (C. 4)) provides for the coming into operation of Articles 10(2) and 50 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”), the enabling provisions under which these Regulations are made, on 6th April 1996 for the purposes only of authorising the making of regulations. As the Regulations are made before the end of a period of six months from the commencement of those Articles, the requirement for consultation under Article 117(1) of the Order does not apply by virtue of paragraph (2)(c) of that Article.

items.title: Plant Health (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Plant Health Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 to provide for the granting of licences by the Department of Agriculture for Northern Ireland in accordance with the requirements of Commission Directive 95/44/EC establishing the conditions under which certain harmful organisms, plants, plant products and other objects listed in Annexes I to V to Council Directive 77/93/EEC may be introduced into or moved within the Community or certain protected zones thereof, for trial or scientific purposes and for work on varietal selections (O.J. No. L.184, 3.8.95, p. 34).

items.title: Legal Advice and Assistance (Prospective Cost) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Legal Advice and Assistance (Prospective Cost) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 to increase from £86·50 to £88·00 the limit on the cost of legal advice and assistance provided under Articles 3 and 4 of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 which a solicitor may incur without obtaining the approval of the appropriate authority.

items.title: The Statutory Maternity Pay (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Statutory Maternity Pay (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 by inserting a new regulation (regulation 2A) which extends the jurisdiction of the Pensions Ombudsman to include complaints against scheme administrators.

items.title: Horse Racing (Charges on Bookmakers) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: Article 9(1) of the Horse Racing (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 provides for charges to be paid to the Department of Agriculture by persons who intend to apply for bookmakers' and bookmaking office licences.

items.title: Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for the payment by the Department of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital. Regulation 2 amends the financial disregards applied in calculating student income.

items.title: Health and Safety (Repeals and Revocations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations repeal the Hours of Employment (Conventions) Act 1936 in so far as it applies to Northern Ireland and Part VIII of the Factories Act (Northern Ireland) 1965. They also revoke certain orders relating to home work.

items.title: Compulsory Acquisition (Interest) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order fixes the rate of interest on compensation money payable under the enactments mentioned in the Order. The rate is 0.5% below the standard rate applying from time to time. The standard rate is the base rate quoted by the seven largest banks authorised under the Banking Act 1987 on the reference day falling on 1st July 1996 and on any subsequent such days.

items.title: Interest on Recoverable Sanitation Expenses Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order fixes the rate of interest on certain expenses recoverable from owners of premises under the Public Health (Ireland) Act 1878. The rate is 0.5% below the standard rate applying from time to time. The standard rate is the base rate quoted by the seven largest banks authorised under the Banking Act 1987 on the reference day falling on 1st July 1996 and on any subsequent such days.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend Order 1 of the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980 to substitute new rules 12 and 17 providing for the allocation of proceedings (including proceedings under the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995) to the Family Division in the High Court and for the allocation of business to the Office of Care and Protection.

items.title: Countryside Access Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of aid to farmers who agree to provide access to the public through their farmland or farm lanes, pursuant to Article 2(1)(g) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (O.J. No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) (“the Council Regulation”). Such aid is part of the zonal programme relating to Northern Ireland submitted by the United Kingdom to the European Commission pursuant to Article 3 of the Council Regulation. The European Commission in accordance with Article 7 of the Council Regulation approved this part of the programme in Commission Decision No. [C] (95) 1678 of 17th August 1995, a copy of which is available from the Department of Agriculture, Room 657, Dundonald House, Upper Newtownards Road, Belfast BT4 3SB.

items.title: Certification Officer (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations increase the fees payable to the Certification Officer under the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 prescribed in the Certification Officer (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 133).

items.title: Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order:

items.title: Gas (1996 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order brings into operation the provisions of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) (Article 2).

items.title: The Protection of Water Against Agricultural Nitrate Pollution Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to Council Directive 91/676/EEC concerning the protection of waters against pollution caused by nitrates from agricultural sources.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support. The new rate is 7·48 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, Regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order appoints 30th May 1996 as the day for the coming into operation of certain provisions of, as modified in its application to Northern Ireland by Schedule 8 to, the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 relating to the definition of disability; past disabilities; guidance; definition of lease, sub-lease and sub-tenancy; advice and assistance; statutory authority and national security; restriction of publicity in industrial tribunals; regulations and orders; interpretation; financial provisions; House of Commons and Northern Ireland Assembly disqualification; provisions supplementing the definition of disability and past disabilities.

items.title: The Litter (Statutory Undertakers) (Designation and Relevant Land) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order makes provision as to the application of the Litter (Northern Ireland) Order 1994 (the Litter Order) to statutory undertakers.

items.title: The Sperrin Lakeland Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This order amends the Sperrin Lakeland Health and Social Services Trust (Establishment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 116) with effect from 3rd June 1996.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987. They provide that the Northern Ireland Housing Executive is required to consider all claims for a rent allowance under regulation 10A (determinations) (regulation 2).

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend the Legal Aid in Criminal Proceedings (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1992 by increasing the rates of remuneration for certain legal aid work in criminal proceedings done on or after 30th June 1996. The Rules also alter the date after which certain work may be remunerated at discretionary instead of prescribed rates from 30th June 1996 to 30th June 1997.

items.title: The Street Works (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 3rd June 1996 the provisions of the Street Works (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 specified in the Schedule.

items.title: The Family Credit (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 by providing that awards of family credit shall be reviewed and shall terminate where persons between the ages of 16 and 19 leave, or have already left, full-time education during the period those awards are in effect and that person is the only member of that household in respect of whom family credit is payable (regulation 2). The Regulations also define what is to constitute full-time education for the purpose of this requirement.

items.title: The Social Security (Disability Living Allowance, Attendance Allowance and Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”), the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: Offshore Installations and Wells (Design and Construction, etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
The Suckler Cow Premium (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
The Air Quality Standards (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 7 of the Air Quality Standards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1990 (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994, as amended. They comply with Council Directiive 75/268/EEC on mountain and hill farming and farming in less-favoured areas, as amended by Council Directive 80/666/EEC and Council Directive 82/786/EEC and with Articles 17 to 19 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2328/91 on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 870/93, Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1992/93 and Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3669/93. The Regulations also make provision for the administration and enforcement of provisions for payment of compensatory allowances contained in Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3887/92 laying down detailed rules for applying the integrated administration and control system for certain community aid schemes.

items.title: Game Birds Preservation Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prohibits, except where exemption is granted, the killing or taking of partridge, red-legged partridges and hen pheasants during the normal open season for game birds. It also restricts dealings in partridge, red-legged partridges or hen pheasants and prohibits dealings in grouse.

items.title: Planning (General Development) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Planning (General Development) Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 to permit, subject to certain qualifications and restrictions—

items.title: On-Street Parking (Amendment) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These bye-laws amend the On-Street Parking Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1987 by revising the charges applicable to on-street parking.

items.title: Off-Street Parking Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Bye-Laws revoke and re-enact with minor amendments the Off-Street Parking Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1994 and all subsequent amending bye-laws.

items.title: Diseases of Animals (Modification) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further modifies Part II of Schedule 2 to the Diseases of Animals (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the principal Order”) which prescribes the amount of compensation payable for cattle slaughtered or caused to be slaughtered as a result of being affected with bovine spongiform encephalopathy or suspected of being so affected and cattle which are in contact with cattle affected by the disease or which appear to be otherwise exposed to the infection of that disease.

items.title: Brucellosis Control (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Brucellosis Control Order (Northern Ireland) 1972 (“the principal Order”) which prescribes the amount of compensation payable for bovine animals which are slaughtered or caused to be slaughtered by the Department because they are reactors when tested for brucellosis or have been exposed to the possibility of infection with the disease.

items.title: The Social Security (Adjudication) and Child Support (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the Adjudication Regulations”) and the Child Support Appeal Tribunals (Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the Child Support Regulations”).

items.title: Tuberculosis Control (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Tuberculosis Control Order (Northern Ireland) 1964 (“the principal Order”) which prescribes the amount of compensation payable for bovine animals which are slaughtered or caused to be slaughtered by the Department because they are reactors when tested for tuberculosis or have been exposed to the possibility of infection with the disease.

items.title: Parking Places on Roads (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Parking Places on Roads Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the 1993 Order”).

items.title: Seed Potatoes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Seed Potatoes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981 by removing the requirement to mark the name of the variety on packages and containers of basic seed potatoes produced in Northern Ireland which are exported or intended to be exported from Northern Ireland.

items.title: Disclosure of Interests in Shares (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for disregarding, for the purposes of the disclosure of interests in shares pursuant to Articles 206 to 210 of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, certain interests in shares arising from arrangements effected under a computer-based system for the transfer of title to securities otherwise than by a written instrument where the operator of the system is approved by the Treasury under the Uncertificated Securities Regulations 1995 (“the system”).

items.title: Equipment and Protective Systems Intended for Use in Potentially Explosive Atmospheres Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations implement as respects Northern Ireland the European Parliament and the Council Directive No. 94/9/EC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States concerning equipment and protective systems intended for use in potentially explosive atmospheres (the ATEX Directive). Subject to certain limited exceptions, references in the Regulations to the Community or a Member State include respectively a reference to the EEA or an EEA State, which are defined in regulation 2(1)(b).

items.title: Planning (Use Classes) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Planning (Use Classes) Order (Northern Ireland) 1989 by omitting Classes 7 to 10 (Special Industrial) from the Schedule.

items.title: Plant Health (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order further amends the Plant Health Order (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: The Registered Rents (Increase) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order increases the rents of regulated tenancies registered with the Department of the Environment by 2.75 per cent from 4th March 1996. Under Article 22(4) of the Rent (Northern Ireland) Order 1978 a notice of increase of rent which gives effect to such increases shall not take effect earlier than 4 weeks after the commencement of this Order, i.e., not earlier than 1st April 1996.

items.title: Genetically Modified Organisms (Contained Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Genetically Modified Organisms (Contained Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) in the following ways—

items.title: Offshore, and Pipelines, Safety (1992 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 14th July 1996 the last remaining provision, paragraph (3) of Article 4, of the Offshore, and Pipelines, Safety (Northern Ireland) Order 1992. That paragraph, as amended, provides that certain Articles of, and Schedules to, the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996, and any Regulations made or having effect under those provisions, shall have effect as if they were “existing statutory provisions” of the Health and Safety at Work (Northern Ireland) Order 1978.

items.title: Financial Markets and Insolvency Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: The Regulations apply with modifications certain provisions of Part V of the Companies (No. 2) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1504 (N.I. 10)) (“Part V”) to certain charges (and to property subject to those charges) granted in favour of those who undertake assured payment obligations in connection with the settlement of transactions through a relevant system in respect of which an Operator has been approved under the Uncertificated Securities Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3272). The provisions applied are ones which modify the way in which the charges in question are treated in the event of an administration or certain kinds of receivership under the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/2405 (N.I. 19)) and ones which modify the way in which rights of persons other than the chargee may be exercised in relation to property subject to those charges.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These regulations make minor amendments to regulations 17(12)(b) and 37(9)(b) of the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations 1988 and substitute revised forms of caution for those contained in Schedule 1A to those regulations.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These regulations make a minor amendment to regulation 15(12)(b) of the Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations 1988 and substitute revised forms of caution for those contained in Schedule 1A to those regulations.

items.title: Arable Area Payments (Grazing of Bovine Animals on Set-Aside Land) (Temporary Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision as respects Northern Ireland for the implementation of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1091/96 which authorises the United Kingdom, until 31st August 1996, to permit the grazing of bovine animals of more than 30 months on land set aside under Article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1765/92 establishing a support system for producers of certain arable crops.

items.title: Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feeding Stuffs Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) and implement as respects Northern Ireland the following European Community Legislation:

items.title: The Jobseekers (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation on 5th February 1996 of provisions in the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) authorising the making of regulations.

items.title: Teachers' Superannuation (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of additional voluntary contributions by teachers who are members of the Teachers' Superannuation Scheme as constituted by the Teacher’s Superannuation Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1977, (“the Principal Regulations”) in order to secure additional benefits financed by investment of those contributions.

items.title: Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (Codes of Practice) (No. 2) Order 1996
items.description: This order appoints 29th July 1996 as the date on which the code of practice under Article 60 and the revised codes of practice under Article 65 of the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 will come into operation.

items.title: The Marking of Plastic Explosive for Detection Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
Food Safety (Fishery Products and Live Bivalve Molluscs and Other Shellfish) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make miscellaneous amendments to the Food Safety (Fishery Products) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993, the Food Safety (Fishery Products on Fishing Vessels) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993, the Food Safety (Live Bivalve Molluscs and Other Shellfish) (Import Conditions and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 and the Food Safety (Fishery Products) (Import Conditions and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995. The majority of the amendments implement Commission Decisions concerning the import conditions for fishery products and live bivalve molluscs and other shellfish from specified countries outside the European Economic Area. It is an offence to import a product unless the requirements of those Decisions are complied with.

items.title: Registration of Deeds (Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order which revokes the Registration of Deeds (Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 1995, specifies the fees to be taken in respect of documents lodged for Registration in the Registry of Deeds and for other work carried out by that registry.

items.title: The Juries (1996 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order appoints 31st July 1996 for the coming into operation of the Juries (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: Form of Jurors Oath Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of oath to be taken by jurors in the Crown Court.

items.title: Juries Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations prescribe:

items.title: Traffic Weight Restriction (Millbay Road, Islandmagee) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order prohibits vehicles exceeding 7.5 tonnes maximum gross weight from using Millbay Road, Route B90, Islandmagee, County Antrim.

items.title: The Northern Ireland Disability Council (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the Department of Health and Social Services to commission research at the request of the Northern Ireland Disability Council established by section 50 of, as modified in its application to Northern Ireland by Schedule 8 to, the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (regulation 2). They also prescribe the circumstances in which the Council may appoint advisers, and the conditions subject to which such appointments are to be made (regulation 3).

items.title: Diseases of Animals (Modification No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order modifies Schedule 1 to the Diseases of Animals (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”). Article 2 adds equine viral arteritis to the list of diseases in Part III of Schedule 1. The effect is to make equine viral arteritis a disease regulated by the 1981 Order.

items.title: Equine Viral Arteritis Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order introduces controls on equine viral arteritis in horses. The Order requires the Department to be notified where a person knows or has reasonable grounds to suspect that a mare which has been served either naturally or by way of artificial insemination within the previous 14 days or a stallion is or may be affected with equine viral arteritis or that a stallion is a carrier of the virus of the disease. It also requires notification where a person reasonably suspects that the disease or the virus exists following analysis of samples of serum or semen (Article 3).

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 as follows—

items.title: Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the Construction Industry Training Board to the Department of Economic Development for the imposition of a further levy upon employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement No. 3 and Saving and Transitional Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation on 11th July 1996 of provisions in the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (“the Act”) authorising the making of Regulations.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1979 principally to take account of the repeal and replacement of the Criminal Justice (Confiscation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 (as amended) by the Proceeds of Crime (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 2) 1996
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980 so as to—

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 3) 1996
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 3) 1996 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Pensions (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This Order brings Article 162 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 into operation on 1st August 1996, with the following exceptions:

items.title: The Jobseekers (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: This, the third Order commencing provisions of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”), provides for the coming into operation on 17th July 1996 of certain provisions in Schedule 2 which make consequential amendments to the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 8).

items.title: Food Safety (General Food Hygiene) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Food Safety (General Food Hygiene) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) so that those Regulations apply to a person carrying on the handling in or sale from any catering establishment or shop premises of ice-cream or heat-treated cream. The 1995 Regulations implement Council Directive 93/43/EEC on the hygiene of foodstuffs (O.J. No. L175, 19.7.93, p. 1), except for the requirements of paragraphs 4 and 5 of Chapter IX of the Annex to that Directive (which relate to temperature controls) and for the requirements in that Directive which relate to imports which are likely to pose a serious risk to human health and which come from countries that are not part of the European Community. They also implement the provisions of Council Directive 80/778/EEC relating to the quality of water intended for human consumption (O.J. No. L229, 30.8.90, p. 11) which relates to the use of water for food production purposes.

items.title: Dairy Products (Hygiene) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Dairy Products (Hygiene) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal Regulations”). These Regulations—

items.title: The Child Benefit, Child Support and Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations, in particular regulation 2(2), amend the Child Benefit and Social Security (Fixing and Adjustment of Rates) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1976 (“the Child Benefit Rates Regulations”) so as to specify a composite rate of child benefit to be payable in respect of the only, elder or eldest child of a lone parent rather than two distinct rates.

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Jobseeker’s Allowance) (Consequential Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the regulations listed below. The amendments are consequential on the coming into operation of the Jobseekers (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”), which replaces income support for the unemployed and unemployment benefit with jobseeker’s allowance. The Regulations replace references to unemployment benefit with references to jobseeker’s allowance, and add references to jobseeker’s allowance where the existing regulations refer to income support.

items.title: The Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992. They provide for a further exemption from the provisions of regulation 12A of those Regulations (hospitalisation in mobility component cases) (as inserted by regulation 4(3) of the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance, Attendance Allowance and Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996) by providing that certain persons residing in hospices shall continue to receive the mobility component of disability living allowance.

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence (Application to Customs and Excise) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
items.description: In consequence of the abolition of certain civil service grades and the introduction of job titles and a pay-band system for officers of HM Customs and Excise, this Order amends the Police and Criminal Evidence (Application to Customs and Excise) Order (Northern Ireland) 1989 by applying the equivalent job titles and pay-banding, as appropriate, of officers of HM Customs and Excise to equivalent ranks of constables and police officers.

items.title: County Courts (Amendment) (1996 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1996
