items.description: An Act to make provision for elections in Northern Ireland for the purpose of providing delegates from among whom participants in negotiations may be drawn; for a forum constituted by those delegates; for referendums in Northern Ireland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Rating (Caravans and Boats) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about liability for non-domestic rates in England and Wales in relation to certain caravans and boats.

items.title: Non-Domestic Rating (Information) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with the disclosure by persons who are valuation officers or assessors to other such persons of information connected with non-domestic rating.

items.title: Reserve Forces Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the reserve forces of the Crown and persons liable to be recalled for permanent service; to amend the provisions of the Reserve Forces Act 1980 relating to the lieutenancies; to amend the law relating to the postponement of the discharge or transfer to the reserve of regular servicemen; and for connected purposes.

items.title: National Health Service (Residual Liabilities) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the transfer of liabilities of certain National Health Service bodies in the event of their ceasing to exist.

items.title: Police Act 1996
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Police Act 1964, Part IX of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984, Chapter I of Part I of the Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994 and certain other enactments relating to the police.

items.title: Employment Tribunals Act 1996
items.description: An Act to consolidate enactments relating to industrial tribunals and the Employment Appeal Tribunal.

items.title: Employment Rights Act 1996
items.description: An Act to consolidate enactments relating to employment rights.

items.title: Law Reform (Year and a Day Rule) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to abolish the “year and a day rule” and, in consequence of its abolition, to impose a restriction on the institution in certain circumstances of proceedings for a fatal offence.

items.title: Hong Kong (Overseas Public Servants) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to confer power to make provision for the making of payments to, and to permit early retirement by, certain Hong Kong overseas public servants; to authorise the provision of resettlement services to certain Hong Kong overseas public servants who retire early; and to confer power to make provision for the making in certain circumstances of payments to supplement pensions and gratuities paid to or in respect of retired overseas public servants in respect of service in Hong Kong.

items.title: Dogs (Fouling of Land) Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to the fouling of land by dogs.

items.title: London Regional Transport Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to extend, and facilitate the exercise of, the powers of London Regional Transport to enter into and carry out agreements; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to re-enact, with omissions and amendments, the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1991; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Arbitration Act 1996
items.description: An Act to restate and improve the law relating to arbitration pursuant to an arbitration agreement; to make other provision relating to arbitration and arbitration awards; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Treasure Act 1996
items.description: An Act to abolish treasure trove and to make fresh provision in relation to treasure.

items.title: Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about criminal procedure and criminal investigations.

items.title: Offensive Weapons Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about persons having knives, other articles which have a blade or are sharply pointed or offensive weapons; and about selling knives or such articles to persons under the age of sixteen years.

items.title: Family Law Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to: divorce and separation; legal aid in connection with mediation in disputes relating to family matters; proceedings in cases where marriages have broken down; rights of occupation of certain domestic premises; prevention of molestation; the inclusion in certain orders under the Children Act 1989 of provisions about the occupation of a dwelling-house; the transfer of tenancies between spouses and persons who have lived together as husband and wife; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1996 (repealed 8.12.1999)
items.description: An Act to amend section 2 of the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1978 to confer further powers on the Commonwealth Development Corporation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sexual Offences (Conspiracy and Incitement) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about conspiracy, or incitement, to commit certain sexual acts outside the United Kingdom.

items.title: Wild Mammals (Protection) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision for the protection of wild mammals from certain cruel acts; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Community Care (Direct Payments) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to enable local authorities responsible for community care services to make payments to persons in respect of their securing the provision of such services; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Defamation Act 1996
items.description: An Act to amend the law of defamation and to amend the law of limitation with respect to actions for defamation or malicious falsehood.

items.title: Trading Schemes Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision in respect of certain trading schemes; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Prisoners’ Earnings Act 1996
items.description: An Act to authorise deductions from or levies on prisoners' earnings; to provide for the application of such deductions or levies; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Marriage Ceremony (Prescribed Words) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to provide alternatives for the declaration and words of contract prescribed by law for marriage ceremonies in registered buildings and register offices, on approved premises and in certain other circumstances.

items.title: Security Service Act 1996
items.description: An Act to give the Security Service the function of acting in support of the prevention and detection of serious crime, and for connected purposes.

items.title: Licensing (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 to require licensing boards to attach to licences conditions relating to certain events involving music and dancing and to make new provision for the composition of licensing boards for licensing divisions.

items.title: Noise Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about noise emitted from dwellings at night; about the forfeiture and confiscation of equipment used to make noise unlawfully; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Energy Conservation Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make further provisions for energy conservation; and for related purposes.

items.title: Civil Aviation (Amendment) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to amend the Civil Aviation Act 1982 so as to provide for the prosecution of persons committing offences on foreign aircraft while in flight to the United Kingdom; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consolidated Fund Act 1996 (repealed 16.7.1998)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1995 and 1996.

items.title: Party Wall etc. Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision in respect of party walls, and excavation and construction in proximity to certain buildings or structures; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Hong Kong (War Wives and Widows) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to provide for the acquisition of British citizenship by certain women who are Hong Kong residents.

items.title: Railway Heritage Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make further provision for and in connection with the preservation of railway records and artefacts.

items.title: Education (Scotland) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to provide for the establishment of a body corporate to be known as the Scottish Qualifications Authority; to provide for the transfer of functions, property, rights, liabilities, obligations and staff to that body and for the conferring of other functions on it; to make provision enabling payment of grant to providers of education for children under school age; to amend certain legislation relating to school education in Scotland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Deer (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 1996 (repealed 19.11.1996)
items.description: An Act to amend the Deer (Scotland) Act 1959; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Appropriation Act 1996 (repealed 16.7.1998)
items.description: An Act to apply a sum out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending on 31st March 1997; to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament; and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: Armed Forces Act 1996
items.description: An Act to continue the Army Act 1955, the Air Force Act 1955 and the Naval Discipline Act 1957; to amend those Acts and other enactments relating to the armed forces; to make further provision in relation to proceedings before service courts; to provide for the taking of fingerprints and samples from offenders convicted in service proceedings; to amend the Courts-Martial (Appeals) Act 1968; to make further provision in relation to complaints to industrial tribunals by members of the armed forces; to provide for further exemptions from the Firearms Act 1968; to make further provision in relation to Greenwich Hospital; to amend the Visiting Forces Act 1952; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Trusts of Land and Appointment of Trustees Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make new provision about trusts of land including provision phasing out the Settled Land Act 1925, abolishing the doctrine of conversion and otherwise amending the law about trusts for sale of land; to amend the law about the appointment and retirement of trustees of any trust; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Damages Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make new provision in relation to damages for personal injury, including injury resulting in death.

items.title: Asylum and Immigration Act 1996
items.description: An Act to amend and supplement the Immigration Act 1971 and the Asylum and Immigration Appeals Act 1993; to make further provision with respect to persons subject to immigration control and the employment of such persons; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Health Service Commissioners (Amendment) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about the Health Service Commissioners.

items.title: Nursery Education and Grant-Maintained Schools Act 1996
items.description: An Act to provide for the making of grants in respect of nursery education and to permit borrowing by grant-maintained schools.

items.title: Social Security (Overpayments) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to amend section 71 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and section 69 of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992.

items.title: Housing Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about housing, including provision about the social rented sector, houses in multiple occupation, landlord and tenant matters, the administration of housing benefit, the conduct of tenants, the allocation of housing accommodation by local housing authorities and homelessness; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision for grants and other assistance for housing purposes and about action in relation to unfit housing; to amend the law relating to construction contracts and architects; to provide grants and other assistance for regeneration and development and in connection with clearance areas; to amend the provisions relating to home energy efficiency schemes; to make provision in connection with the dissolution of urban development corporations, housing action trusts and the Commission for the New Towns; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Statutory Instruments (Production and Sale) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision (with retrospective effect) for the printing and sale of statutory instruments under the authority of the Queen’s printer, for their issue under the authority of Her Majesty’s Stationery Office and for the reception in evidence of lists of such instruments which do not bear the imprint of the Queen’s printer.

items.title: Broadcasting Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make new provision about the broadcasting in digital form of television and sound programme services and the broadcasting in that form on television or radio frequencies of other services; to amend the Broadcasting Act 1990; to make provision about rights to televise sporting or other events of national interest; to amend in other respects the law relating to the provision of television and sound programme services; to provide for the establishment and functions of a Broadcasting Standards Commission and for the dissolution of the Broadcasting Complaints Commission and the Broadcasting Standards Council; to make provision for the transfer to other persons of property, rights and liabilities of the British Broadcasting Corporation relating to their transmission network; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Education Act 1996
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Education Act 1944 and certain other enactments relating to education, with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission.

items.title: School Inspections Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to consolidate provisions of the Education (Schools) Act 1992 and Part V of the Education Act 1993, with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission.

items.title: Deer (Scotland) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to consolidate the legislation relating to deer in Scotland.

items.title: Public Order (Amendment) Act 1996 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the power of arrest of section 5 of the Public Order Act 1986.

items.title: Chemical Weapons Act 1996
items.description: An Act to promote the control of chemical weapons and of certain toxic chemicals and precursors; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consolidated Fund (No. 2) Act 1996 (repealed 16.7.1998)
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service  of the years ending on 31st March 1997 and 1998.

items.title: Channel Tunnel Rail Link Act 1996
items.description: An Act to provide for the construction, maintenance and operation of a railway between St. Pancras, in London, and the Channel Tunnel portal at Castle Hill, Folkestone, in Kent, together with associated works, and of works which can be carried out in conjunction therewith; to make provision about related works; to provide for the improvement of the A2 at Cobham, in Kent, and of the M2 between junctions 1 and 4, together with associated works; to make provision with respect to compensation in relation to the acquisition of blighted land; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Theft (Amendment) Act 1996
items.description: An Act to amend the Theft Act 1968 and the Theft Act 1978; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office Act 1996
items.description: An Act to make provision about privileges and immunities in relation to an economic and trade office established in the United Kingdom by the government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

items.title: Prevention of Terrorism (Additional Powers) Act 1996(repealed)
items.description: An Act to extend powers of search in connection with acts of terrorism and terrorist investigations; confer powers on constables in relation to areas on which police cordons are imposed in connection with terrorist investigations; and confer powers in connection with the prevention of acts of terrorism to impose prohibitions and restrictions in relation to vehicles on roads.

items.title: Finance Act 1996
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with Finance.

items.title: Education (Student Loans) Act 1996 (repealed 13.8.1998)
items.description: An Act to make provision for, and in consequence of, the payment of subsidy in respect of private sector student loans.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Pension Business)(Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insurance Companies (Pensions Business) (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/2326) (“the principal Regulations”) which were made under powers contained in Schedule 19AB to the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“Schedule 19AB” and “the 1988 Act” respectively). Schedule 19AB provides that insurance companies carrying on pension business shall be entitled to provisional repayments of tax on pension business investment income.

items.title: The Bexley Community Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1996
The Norfolk and Norwich Health Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1996
The East Yorkshire Community Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1996
The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 1995 (“the Document”) by uprating the amounts of salary and certain allowances. The following are increased:

items.title: The Registers of Scotland Executive Agency Trading Fund Order 1996
items.description: This Order provides for the setting up as from 1st April 1996 of a fund with public money under the Government Trading Funds Act 1973 to finance the operations of the Department of the Registers of Scotland, now known as the Registers of Scotland Executive Agency, described in Schedule 1 to the Order. It also provides for the fund to be under the control and management of the Keeper of the Registers of Scotland. The Order designates the National Loans Fund as the authorised lender to the fund and imposes a limit of £40,000,000 on the amount which may be lent to it. It provides for the assets (estimated at £13,763,000) and liabilities (estimated at £3,715,000) set out in Schedule 2 to the Order to be appropriated to the fund and for 40% of the amount by which the value of the assets exceeds the value of the liabilities to be treated as public dividend capital.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Districts (Alteration of Boundaries) Order 1996
items.description: The Sheriff Court Districts Reorganisation Order 1975 (“the 1975 Order”) divided Scotland’s six sheriffdoms into 50 sheriff court districts (one of which was subsequently abolished). This Order alters the boundaries of 6 of these districts with effect from 1st April 1996.

items.title: The Sheriffdoms (Alteration of Boundaries) Order 1996
items.description: By virtue of the Sheriffdoms Reorganisation Order 1974 (“the 1974 Order”), Scotland is divided into six sheriffdoms. This Order alters the boundaries of two of these sheriffdoms, so as to transfer an area around Chryston from the sheriffdom of Glasgow and Strathkelvin into the sheriffdom of South Strathclyde, Dumfries and Galloway. The boundaries of the other four sheriffdoms remain unchanged.

items.title: The Regional Flood Defence Committee (Welsh Region) (Amendment) Order 1996
items.description: This Order revokes the Welsh National Water Development Authority (Regional Land Drainage Committee) Order 1973 (S.I. 1973/1924), consequent upon the provisions of the Regional Flood Defence Committee (Welsh Region) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/538).

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Wales) (Consequential Amendments No. 2) Order 1996
The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Prescribed Proceedings) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Prescribed Proceedings) Regulations 1994 by adding to the proceedings in connection with which criminal legal aid is not to be available. The proceedings are those to which assistance by way of representation under Part II of the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 is made available by regulation 3 of the Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1011).

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Construction Board) Order 1996
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations increase the disposable income limit for eligibility for advice and assistance under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 from £156 a week to £162 a week; they increase the weekly disposable income above which a person is required to pay a contribution from £64 to £67, and they prescribe the scale of contributions to be paid where the weekly disposable income exceeds £67 but does not exceed £162.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations increase certain of the financial limits of eligibility for civil legal aid under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The Lloyd’s Underwriters (Gilt-edged Securities) (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Gilt-edged Securities (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Industrial Training Levy (Engineering Construction Board) Order 1996
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Engineering Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the engineering construction industry (including employers who are not mainly engaged in engineering construction activities but are engaged in any related or administrative activities of a kind to which paragraph 1(c) of Schedule 1 to the Industrial Training (Engineering Board) Order 1964 (as amended by the Industrial Training (Engineering Construction Board) Order 1991) applies; and for those employers the levy will be imposed only in respect of the said related or administrative activities for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal (Fees) Rules 1996
items.description: These Rules provide for a new scale of fees to be taken in proceedings before the Lands Tribunal, following the reovcation of the Lands Tribunal Rules 1975 (S.I. 1975/299) by the Lands Tribunal Rules 1996, which contain the rules of procedure of the Tribunal. The 1975 Rules incorported both rules of procedure and fees.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal Rules 1996
items.description: These Rules make provision for the procedure to be followed in respect of cases before the Lands Tribunal. They replace the Lands Tribunal Rules 1975. The Rules consolidate the procedure in relation to appeals. They no longer provided for prescribed forms to be used except in relation to applications under section 2 of the Right of Light Act 1959. The main changes to the general procedure of the Tribunal are—

items.title: The Employment Protection (Continuity of Employment of National Health Service Employees) (Modification) Order 1996
items.description: The Order provides that a doctor or dentist in the training grades of registrar, senior registrar or specialist registrar will have his or her employment with a health service employer treated as being continuous for the purposes of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 when he or she has moved to a different health service employer.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Committees (Bolton, Bury, Rochdale, Salford and Wigan) Amalgamation Order 1996
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the magistrates' courts committees of Bolton, Bury, Rochdale, Salford, and Wigan and provides for the replacement of those committees by a single committee to be known as the North and West Greater Manchester magistrates' courts committee.

items.title: The A501 Trunk Road (Marylebone Road, Westminster) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) (No. 2) Order 1996
The Superannuation (Admission to Schedule 1 of the Superannuation Act 1972) (No. 2) Order 1996
The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in respect of property in Scotland. They apply only to financial year 1996-97 (“the relevant year”).

items.title: The Environment Act 1995 (Isles of Scilly) Order 1996
The Capital Gains Tax (Gilt-edged Securities) Order 1996
items.description: This Order specifies gilt-edged securities disposals of which are exempt from tax on capital gains in accordance with section 115 of the Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”).

items.title: The North Eastern Sea Fisheries District (Constitution of Committee and Expenses) (Variation) Order 1996
items.description: This Order varies those provisions of the North Eastern Sea Fisheries District Order 1986 which relate to the constitution of the Committee and the expenses payable by the constituent authorities, in consequence of the changes to the local Government areas contained in the Cleveland (Structural Change) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995 No. 187) and the Humberside (Structural Change) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995 No. 600).

items.title: The Fishing Boats (Specified Countries) Designation Order 1996 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates the Faroe Islands and Norway as countries whose registered fishing boats may fish for certain species of sea fish in certain areas of British fishery limits outside the area within 12 miles from the baselines from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured.

items.title: The Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, which replaces and revokes the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1995, as amended by the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) (Amendment) Order 1995, provides for the enforcement of Community Regulations which authorise fishing in certain specified areas within member States' fishery limits for specified descriptions of fish by vessels of third countries, namely Norway and the Faroe Islands, in 1996. These Regulations contain requirements concerning fishing quotas and authorised zones, methods of fishing, the holding of licences and observance of licence conditions, the keeping of log books, the making of radio reports and similar matters.

items.title: The Beef (Emergency Control) (Amendment) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Beef (Emergency Control) Order 1996 to enable meat from bovine animals with more than one pair of incisors to be sold if the animal can be shown by reference to a national administrative document to be no more than two years and six months old. It also clarifies the dentition criterion.

items.title: The A23 Trunk Road (Purley Cross Junction Improvement) Trunking Order 1996
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Reduced Rates of Class 1 Contributions) (Salary Related Contracted-out Schemes) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by a resolution of each House of Parliament, is made as a consequence of a review by the Secretary of State under section 42 of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) following a report by the Government Actuary in relation to the contracted-out percentages under section 41 of the 1993 Act.

items.title: The Social Security (Reduced Rates of Class 1 Contributions and Rebates) (Money Purchase Contracted-out Schemes) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by a resolution of each House of Parliament, is made as a consequence of a review by the Secretary of State under section 42B of the Pension Schemes Act  (“the 1993 Act”) following a report by the Government Actuary in relation to the contracted-out percentages under section 42 of the 1993 Act.

items.title: The Social Security (Minimum Contributions to Appropriate Personal Pension Schemes) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by a resolution of each House of Parliament, is made as a consequence of a review by the Secretary of State under section 45A of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) following a report by the Government Actuary in relation to minimum contributions under section 43 of the 1993 Act.

items.title: The Tyne Riverside Enterprise Zones (North Tyneside) (Designation) (No. 1) Order 1996
The Licensing (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1996
The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Stockport Borough Council) Order 1996
items.description: Under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (competition), work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if certain conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The New Town (Glenrothes) Dissolution Order 1996
The New Town (East Kilbride) Dissolution Order 1996
items.description: This Order appoints 5th April 1996 as the date for the dissolution of the East Kilbride Development Corporation.

items.title: The Vehicle Excise Duty (Immobilisation, Removal and Disposal of Vehicles) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations provide for the immobilisation of unlicensed mechanically propelled vehicles found stationary on public roads in the designated clamping areas referred to in Schedule 2. Regulation 5 enables a person authorised by the Secretary of State to fix an immobilisation device to such a vehicle. Regulation 6 states the conditions to be fulfilled (including the payment of prescribed charges specified in Schedule 1) for the release of a vehicle from an immobilisation device and regulation 7 sets out the exemptions from immobilisation. Offences in connection with immobilisation (including unlawful interference with an immobilisation device and falsely claiming exemption to secure the release of a vehicle) are created by regulations 8 and 9.

items.title: The Statutory Nuisance (Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision as regards Scotland with respect to appeals to the sheriff against abatement notices served under section 80 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (as amended by the Noise and Statutory Nuisance Act 1993) and those served under section 80A of the 1990 Act (as inserted by the 1993 Act).

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Gloucester Place/Ivor Place, Westminster) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Grays Inn Road, Camden) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The Buying Agency Trading Fund (Amendment) Order 1996
The A406 Trunk Road (Ealing and Hounslow) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Ealing) Red Route (Prescribed Routes and Turns No 1) Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Hounslow) Red Route (Prescribed Route No. 1) Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The Beef (Emergency Control) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Beef (Emergency Control) Order 1996, as amended, to exclude from the scope of the Order meat from bovine animals slaughtered in specified countries.

items.title: The Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The North of Scotland Milk Marketing Board Dissolution Order 1996
items.description: This Order provides that the North of Scotland Milk Marketing Board shall be dissolved on 29th April 1996.

items.title: The Aberdeen and District Milk Marketing Board Dissolution Order 1996
The Bath-Lincoln Trunk Road A46 (Upper Swainswick to A420 Cold Ashton Roundabout) Orders 1987 Revocation Order 1996
The National Disability Council Regulations 1996
The A629 Trunk Road (Skipton To Kildwick Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1996
The A629 Trunk Road (Ings Lane to Cononley Lane) (Detrunking) Order 1996
The Prohibition of Keeping of Live Fish (Crayfish) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, which is made under section 1 of the Import of Live Fish (England and Wales) Act 1980 (“the Act”), and which comes into force on 29 May 1996, prohibits, subject to an exception, the keeping in England and Wales of any species of crayfish other than the native species, opotamobius pallipes except under authority of a licence granted by the Minister. There is an exception to the prohibition in respect of crayfish of the species stacus leniusculus(signal crayfish) which are kept within those parts of England and Wales which are described by reference to postal districts in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Companies (Principal Business Activities) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Companies (Forms Amendment No. 2 and Company’s Type and Principal Business Activities) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”). The 1990 Regulations prescribe the system of classifying business activities for the purposes of section 364(1)(b) of the Companies Act 1985 (as substituted by the Companies Act 1989) which requires a company to give its principal business activities in its annual return. These Regulations amend the 1990 Regulations by prescribing (regulation 2) for this purpose the Standard Industrial Classification of Economic Activities 1992. Copies of the Standard Industrial Classification of Economic Activities 1992 are available from Her Majesty’s Stationery Office. The amendment made by regulation 2 is subject to a transitional provision in regulation 3. This allows the continued use of the VAT Trade Classification system in an annual return delivered to the Registrar of Companies on or before 5th May 1997.

items.title: The Genetically Modified Organisms (Risk Assessment) (Records and Exemptions) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to section 108(1)(a) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the Act”), which restricts the import and acquisition of genetically modified organisms (“GMOs”).

items.title: The Prohibition of Keeping of Live Fish (Crayfish) (Scotland) Order 1996
The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1996
The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Education (Grant-maintained Special Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Road Traffic (Special Parking Area) (Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames) (Amendment) Order 1996
The Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Animals and Animals Products (Import and Export) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) by replacing Schedules 2, 3, 5 and 6 of those Regulations to take into account changes in Community legislation since those Regulations were passed (regulation 3). The Community instruments containing the changes are:

items.title: The Road Traffic (Special Parking Area) (London Borough of Newham) (Amendment) Order 1996
The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road, Hounslow) (Prohibition Of Use Of Gap In Central Reserve) Order 1996
The Yorkshire Water services Limited (Drought) Order 1996
The Dual-Use and Related Goods (Export Control) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: The Dual-Use and Related Goods (Export Control) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) contain provisions arising from Council Regulation (EC) No. 3381/94 (O.J. No. L 367, 31.12.94, p.1) on the control of exports of dual-use goods, which in turn makes provision in respect of (amongst other things) the goods listed in Annexes I and IV to Council Decision 94/942/CFSP (O.J. No. L 367, 31.12.94, p.8) on the joint action concerning the control of exports of dual-use goods. Schedules 1 and 2 to the principal Regulations reproduce Annexes I and IV.

items.title: The Fertilisers (Mammalian Meat and Bone Meal) Regulations 1996
The Jobseekers Act 1995 (Commencement No.2) Order 1996
The Passenger Car Fuel Consumption (Amendment) Order 1996
The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order 1996
The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Ealing) Red Route (Prescribed Turns No 2) Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Swinton Street, Camden) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Camden and Islington) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Camden, Islington and Westminster) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The Hong Kong (Overseas Public Servants) (Retirement and Compensation) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes provision for the retirement during the year preceding the ending of British sovereignty over Hong Kong of members of Her Majesty’s Overseas Civil Service and Her Majesty’s Overseas Judiciary in the pensionable service of the Government of Hong Kong and for the payment to them of compensation.

items.title: The Hong Kong (Overseas Public Servants) (Continuing Service: Compensation) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes provision for the payment of compensation to members of Her Majesty’s Overseas Civil Service and Her Majesty’s Overseas Judiciary in the pensionable service of the government of Hong Kong who remain in the service of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region after 30th June 1997.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1984 (Jersey) (Revocation) Order 1996
The Maximum Number of Judges Order 1996
The Merchant Shipping (Liability and Compensation for Oil Pollution Damage) (Transitional Provisions) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes transitional provision for the period following entry into force of the 1992 Protocols to the International Convention on Civil Liability for Oil Pollution Damage, 1969 (CLC), and the International Convention on the Establishment of an International Fund for Compensation for Oil Pollution Damage, 1971. This is one of two orders made to implement the 1992 Protocols in the UK. The Merchant Shipping Act 1995 (Appointed Day No.1) Order 1996 brings into force the provisions of the 1995 Act which implement the 1992 CLC and the 1992 Fund Convention (Chapters III and IV of Part VI of the Act respectively) on 30 May 1996. This Order provides that the equivalent Chapters in Schedule 4 to the 1995 Act (which otherwise cease to have effect following that Appointed Day Order) continue to have effect for transitional purposes.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1996
The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Individual Pupil’s Achievements) (Information) Regulations 1993 (“the principal Regulations”) which apply in relation to pupils and former pupils at maintained schools in England.

items.title: The Fresh Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1996
The Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Conduct of Investment Business) Rules Approval Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Marylebone Road/Nottingham Place, Westminster) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The A4 Trunk Road (Hillingdon) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1996
The Plant Health (Great Britain) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1996
The Beef (Emergency Control) (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1996
The A4 Trunk Road (Hounslow) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1996
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire, City of Winchester) Order 1996
items.description: This Order designates the City of Winchester in the county of Hampshire as a permitted parking area and as a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 and applies with modifications various provisions of that Act to the designated area. The Order provides that persons appointed to act as parking adjudicators in London may so act in relation to the designated area and the Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (London) Regulations 1993 are made to apply with modification to parking adjudicators so acting. The Order also makes consequential modifications to the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984.

items.title: Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations replace the Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) Regulations 1984 and other related regulations which are now revoked. The Regulations supplement the changes introduced by the Pensions Act 1995 to Part III of the Pension Schemes Act 1993. They also consolidate and amend those provisions of the 1984 Regulations which remain relevant. The regulations made by virtue of Part III of the Pensions Act 1995 are made within six months of those provisions coming into force and as a consequence there is no requirement to consult. As regards other regulations, the Occupational Pensions Board has agreed that proposals to make regulations need not be referred to it.

items.title: The Armed Forces Act 1991 (Commencement No.2) Order 1996
The Armed Forces (Protection of Children of Service Families) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations contain provisions which supplement Part III of the Armed Forces Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). Part III of the 1991 Act enables the making of assessment orders and protection orders for the purpose of protecting children of service families.

items.title: The London Cab (No. 2) Order 1996
items.description: This Order increases the fares payable for the hiring of a motor cab in the Metropolitan Police District and the City of London in respect of all journeys beginning and ending there. It also increases the extra charge payable for the carriage of additional passengers.

items.title: The Register of County Court Judgments (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Register of County Court Judgments Regulations 1985 so as to allow liability orders (within the meaning of section 33 of the Child Support Act 1991) to be entered on the Register. The scheme applicable for the registration of county court judgments is applied to liability orders with modifications.

items.title: The County Council of Northumberland (Duplicate North Seaton Bridge) Scheme 1995 Confirmation Instrument 1996
The European Investment Bank (Designated International Organisation) Order 1996
items.description: This Order designates the European Investment Bank as a bank for the purposes of the definition of “bank” in section 840A of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (inserted by paragraph 1(1) of Schedule 37 to the Finance Act 1996 (“Schedule 37”)).

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Gilt-edged Securities) (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insurance Companies (Gilt-edged Securities) (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3223) (“the principal Regulations”) to take account of provisions in the Finance Act 1996 (c. 8). They also make amendments of a drafting nature to the principal Regulations.

items.title: The Gilt-edged Securities (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gilt-edged Securities (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3224) to take account of an amendment made to section 51B of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 by paragraph 4 of Schedule 6 to the Finance Act 1996 (c. 8) which provides for interest to be charged to tax at the lower rate instead of at the basic rate in relation to the year 1996-97 and subsequent years of assessment.

items.title: The Lloyd’s Underwriters (Gilt-edged Securities) (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Lloyd’s Underwriters (Gilt-edged Securities) (Periodic Accounting for Tax on Interest) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3225) to take account of an amendment made to section 51B of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 by paragraph 4 of Schedule 6 to the Finance Act 1996 (c. 8) which provides for interest to be charged to tax at the lower rate instead of at the basic rate in relation to the year 1996-97 and subsequent years of assessment.

items.title: The Aberystwyth Harbour Revision Order 1995
The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Interest Relief) Regulations 1982 (S.I. 1982/1236) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Vocational Training (Tax Relief) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Vocational Training (Tax Relief) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/746) (“the principal Regulations”) following legislative changes made by the Finance Act 1996. They also make amendments of a drafting nature to the principal Regulations.

items.title: The North Hull Housing Action Trust (Transfer of Property) Order 1996
items.description: This Order vests in the North Hull Housing Action Trust the housing and other land specified in the Schedule to the Order which is currently vested in the council of the City of Kingston upon Hull (article 2) and makes provision for rent paid to it or to the Council since 27th January 1992 under tenancies of any of the housing (article 3). The property transferred is in the area designated by the North Hull Housing Action Trust (Area and Constitution) Order 1991 which established the North Hull Housing Action Trust and was in error omitted from the North Hull Housing Action Trust (Transfer of Property) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/1) and also from the North Hull Housing Action Trust (Transfer of Property) (No. 2) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/1754).

items.title: The Deregulation (Friendly Societies Act 1992) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Friendly Societies Act 1992 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Deregulation (Credit Unions) Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Credit Unions Act 1979 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A303 Trunk Road (Sparkford To Ilchester Improvement And Slip Roads) (Detrunking) Order 1996
The A303 Trunk Road (Sparkford to Ilchester Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1996
The Specified Bovine Material (No. 2) Order 1996
The Bovine Animals (Enforcement of Community Purchase Scheme) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the enforcement of certain of the requirements of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 716/96, adopting exceptional support measures for the beef market in the United Kingdom. The Regulation authorises the United Kingdom to purchase any bovine animal aged over 30 months which does not exhibit any clinical sign of BSE and which was, during a period of at least three months prior to its sale, present on a holding located on United Kingdom territory. Various requirements are specified as to the slaughter, treatment and disposal of animals subject to the scheme.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 3) Order 1996
items.description: This order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point, around the installations specified in the Schedule to this order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Payments on Account) (Amendment) Order 1996
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Gas Industry Exemption) (Amendment) Order 1996
The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1996
The Children’s Hearings (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1996
The Glasgow School of Art (Scotland) Order of Council 1996
items.description: This Order makes new provision regarding the constitution, functions and powers of the Governors of The Glasgow School of Art, as governing body of that School, and the arrangements to be adopted by it in discharging its functions. It replaces the provisions of the Central Institutions (Scotland) Regulations 1988 so far as these provisions relate to The Glasgow School of Art.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Torch Communications Limited) Order 1996
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Torch Communications Limited a licence on 8 February 1996 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in that Licence in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1995 (Appointed Day No. 1) Order 1996
The Deregulation (Salmon Fisheries (Scotland) Act 1868) Order 1996
items.description: This Order, amends section 18 of the Salmon Fisheries (Scotland) Act 1868 (“the 1868 Act”) which makes it a criminal offence in Scotland to buy, sell, expose for sale or have in possession any salmon roe.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Oil and Chemical Pollution of Salmon and Migratory Trout) (Revocation) Order 1996
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Oil and Chemical Pollution of Fish and Plants (Partial Revocation) Order 1996
The Local Government Reorganisation (Wales) (Staff) (No. 3) Order 1996
items.description: This Order supplements and, to a certain extent supersedes, the Local Government Reorganisation (Wales) (Staff) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/501) (“the principal Order”) and the Local Government Reorganisation (Wales) (Staff) (No. 2) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/905) (“the No. 2 Order”). Those Orders designated, for the purposes of Section 42 of the Local Government (Wales) Act 1994 (transfers of staff) staff of the district and county councils and of the Milford Port Health Authority which, by virtue of the 1994 Act, ceased to exist on 1st April 1996. They also specified a new county or county borough council or a National Park authority as the new employer of such staff from that date.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Members' Interests) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: The Local Authorities (Members' Interests) Regulations 1992 require an elected member of a local authority to give to the local authority of which he is a member written notice of any interest he holds which is described in the Regulations. Regulations 2 to 5 of these Regulations extend with modifications this requirement to—

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Member-nominated Trustees and Directors) Regulations 1996 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations concern the selection and appointment of member-nominated trustees and directors under sections 16 to 21 of the Pensions Act 1995 (c. 26) (“the Act”) and are made before the expiry of the period of six months beginning with the coming into force of the provisions of Part I of the Act by virtue of which they are made. They are accordingly exempt from the requirement in section 120 of the Act for the Secretary of State to consult such persons as he considers appropriate.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (Subsidy) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes provision for the calculation of housing benefit and council tax benefit subsidy payable under the Social Security Administration Act 1992 to authorities administering housing benefit or council tax benefit.

items.title: The General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules Order of Council 1996
items.description: The rules approved by this Order amend the General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Proceedure) Rules 1988 (appended to S.I. 1988/2255). They give effect to the provisions brought into force by the Medical (Professional Preformance) Act 1995 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/271(c.5)) relating to the powers of the General Medical Council, through its appropriate Committees, to permit interim orders for suspension or conditional registration to be made for an initial period of six months rather than the original two months, and to make further interim orders for periods of up to three months at a time.

items.title: The General Medical Council Health Committee (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules Order of Council 1996
items.description: The rules approved by this Order amend the General Medical Council Health Committee (Procedure) Rules 1987 (appended to S.I. 1987/2174). They give effect to the provisions brought into force by the Medical (Professional Performance) Act 1995 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/271 (c.5)) relating to the powers of the General Medical Council, through its appropriate committees—

items.title: Elections (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
items.description: This Order (which extends to Northern Ireland only) provides for the elections of delegates to be held under section 1 of, and Schedule 1 to, the Northern Ireland (Entry to Negotiations, etc.) Act 1996. Under article 4 of the Order, the date of the poll for the elections is 30th May 1996.

items.title: The A501 Trunk Road (Euston Road/Gower Street, Camden) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Marylebone Road/Glentworth Street, Westminster) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The National Park Authorities (Wales) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1996
items.description: Part III of the Environment Act 1995 provides for the establishment by order under section 63 of that Act of National Park authorities which will be the local planning authority for their respective National Parks. By virtue of the National Park Authorities (Wales) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/2803) National Park authorities for the National Parks in Wales were established on 23 November 1995. They became the local planning authorities for their respective National Parks on 1 April 1996.

items.title: The Education (Coleg Normal Bangor Higher Education Corporation) (Dissolution) Order 1996
items.description: This Order dissolves Coleg Normal Bangor Higher Education Corporation and transfers the property, rights and liabilities of that Corporation to University College of North Wales, Bangor (article 3). Contracts of employment of staff of the Corporation are transferred separately (article 4) by applying section 127 of the Education Reform Act 1988 (which is concerned with the transfer of staff to higher education corporations) with appropriate modifications.

items.title: The Income Tax (Unapproved Manufactured Payments) Regulations 1996
The Income Tax (Manufactured Interest) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Manufactured Interest) Regulations 1992 (S.I.1992/2074) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Stock Lending) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Stock Lending) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1299) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Manufactured Overseas Dividends) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Manufactured Overseas Dividends) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/2004) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The A35 Trunk Road (Chideock Morcombelake Bypass) Order 1996
The North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1996
items.description: This Order transfers to the North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 23rd February 1996 trust property formerly held by the North Staffordshire Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, NHS Trust Unit, Room 1W29, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Compensation for Redundancy or Loss of Remuneration) (Education) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for payment of compensation to certain persons adversely affected by local government reorganisation. The persons to whom these Regulations apply are:—

items.title: The Local Government (Superannuation and Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) and the Local Government (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1979 (“the 1979 Regulations”) to take account of the establishment of new area tourist boards under the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994. The Regulations have retrospective effect from 1st April 1996, as authorised by sections 12(1) and 24(3)(c) of the Superannuation Act 1972.

items.title: The Fishing Vessels (Decommissioning) Scheme 1996
items.description: This Scheme provides for the making of grants by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretaries of State respectively concerned with the sea fish industry in Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland, in respect of the decommissioning of vessels registered in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The National Park Authorities (England) Order 1996
items.description: Under section 63 of the Environment Act 1995, the Secretary of State may by order establish for a National Park an authority, known as a National Park authority, to carry out in relation to that Park the functions conferred on such an authority by or under Part III of that Act.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (London Borough of Greenwich) Order 1996
The Social Security (Additional Pension) (Contributions Paid in Error) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations apply to cases where primary Class 1 contributions have been paid in error because the individual concerned was not an employed earner.

items.title: The Finance Act 1996, section 26, (Appointed Day) Order 1996
The Civil Evidence (Family Mediation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (Commencement and Transitional Provision) Order 1996
The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1996
The Hydrocarbon Oil (Designated Markers) Regulations 1996
The Income Support (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations establish a pilot scheme under the Jobseekers Act 1995. The scheme relates to persons who claim income support and who fulfil the criteria in regulation 3 as to age, the period over which such persons have been receiving benefit (as defined) continuously and location of appropriate offices at which such persons are required to attend (as identified in the Schedule (“relevant persons”).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Fiscal Warehousing) (Treatment of Transactions) Order 1996
items.description: This Order treats for the purposes of VAT certain supplies as being of goods where they would otherwise technically be supplies of services. This enables them to take advantage of the relief from VAT afforded to trade in certain commodities under a fiscal warehousing regime.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Cultural Services) Order 1996
The Civil Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1996
The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1996
The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987 by replacing the part relating to driving tests and making certain other incidental and minor amendments.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Medicines (Animal Feeding Stuffs) (Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st June 1996, further amend the Medicines (Animal Feeding Stuffs) (Enforcement) Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”), and implement:

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1996
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Order 1992 by adding a new Part 25 to Schedule 1 to grant permitted development rights for the installation, alteration or replacement on a building or other structure of a closed circuit television camera for security purposes. Conditions are attached relating to the size and number of cameras permitted, their field of vision and their positioning. A camera must be sited so as to minimise its effect on the external appearance of the building or structure and must be removed after it is no longer required for security purposes.

items.title: The Churnet Valley Light Railway Order 1996
The Charities (Trustee Investments Act 1961) Order 1996
The Housing (Change of Landlord) (Payment of Disposal Cost by Instalments) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1996
The Worthing and Southlands Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1996
items.description: This Order transfers to the Worthing and Southlands Hospitals National Health Service Trust on 23rd February 1996 trust property formerly held by the West Sussex Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, NHS Trust Unit, Room 1W29, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Internal Dispute Resolution Procedures) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations are made before the expiry of the period of six months beginning with the coming into force of the provisions of Part I of the Pensions Act 1995 (c. 26) (“the Act”) by virtue of which they are made. They are accordingly exempt from the requirement in section 120(2)(c) of the Act for the Secretary of State to consult such persons as he considers appropriate.

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Pensions Ombudsman) Regulations 1991 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No.19) (Scotland) Order 1996
The Waste Management Licensing (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend paragraph 17 of Schedule 3 to the Waste Management Licensing Regulations 1994 which exempts from waste management licensing the storage of certain specified kinds of waste where certain conditions are satisfied. The amendment adds two further kinds of waste: mammalian protein and mammalian tallow. Both are products of the rendering of animal carcasses and are defined in the amended paragraph.

items.title: The Redundant Mineworkers (Payments Schemes) (Amendment and Consolidation) Order 1996
items.description: This Order establishes under the Coal Industry Act 1977 a Scheme which amends and consolidates the Schemes established by the Redundant Mineworkers and Concessionary Coal (Payments Schemes) Orders 1983,1984 and 1986 (the “earlier schemes”). The earlier schemes are for the payment of benefits to mineworkers, workers at coking plants and other coal industry employees made redundant (1) on or after 6th April 1983 and before 1 st April 1984 (2) on or after 1st April 1984 and before 30th March 1986 and (3) on or after 30th March 1986 and before 29th March 1987. The Order (subject to certain transitional provisions) revokes and supersedes the earlier schemes.

items.title: The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (Partnership and Co-operation Agreement between the European Communities and their Member States and the Republic of Belarus) Order 1996 (revoked)
The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (Partnership and Co-operation Agreement between the European Communities and their Member States and the Republic of Kazakhstan) Order 1996 (revoked)
The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (Partnership and Co-operation Agreement between the European Communities and their Member States and the Kyrgyz Republic) Order 1996 (revoked)
The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (Partnership and Co-operation Agreement between the European Communities and their Member States and the Republic of Moldova) Order 1996 (revoked)
The Hong Kong (Overseas Public Servants) (Pension Supplements) Order 1996
items.description: This Order provides for the making of certain supplementary payments to overseas public servants and their dependants if their income from Hong Kong service or dependant’s pensions, or if the value of pensionable officers’ gratuities, is reduced as a consequence of a significant fall in the exchange rate between the Hong Kong dollar and the pound sterling.

items.title: The International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund 1992 (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1996
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund 1992, on representatives of its members and on its officers and experts. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with an Agreement which has been negotiated between the Government of the United Kingdom and the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund 1992 (Cm 3241). The Order will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to the Agreement, which will enter into force on signature. The Order will come into force on the date on which the Agreement enters into force.

items.title: The United Nations (International Tribunal) (Rwanda) Order 1996
items.description: This Order makes provision as respects the United Kingdom to implement a resolution of the Security Council of the United Nations relating to Rwanda. The Order has effect for the purpose of enabling the United Kingdom to co-operate with the International Criminal Tribunal for the Prosecution of Persons Responsible for Genocide and Other Serious Violations of International Humanitarian Law Committed in the Territory of Rwanda and Rwandan citizens responsible for genocide and other such violations committed in the territory of neighbouring states, between 1 January 1994 and 31 December 1994, established by resolution 955(1994) of the Security Council of the United Nations (“International Tribunal”) in the investigation and prosecution of persons accused of committing International Tribunal crimes and the punishment of persons convicted of such crimes.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 (Modification) Order 1996
items.description: This Order adds to Part III of Schedule 2 to the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 (which specifies the Class C drugs which are subject to control under the Act) the anabolic and androgenic steroids and derivatives; an andrenoceptor stimulant; and polypeptide hormones specified in article 2(2)(b).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Amendment) Order 1996
The Lotteries (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations apply the scheme established by the Income Support (Pilot Scheme) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1252) to persons claiming a jobseeker’s allowance. The scheme relates to persons who have been claiming income support, jobseeker’s allowance or a combination of benefits and who fulfil the criteria in regulation 3 as to age, the period over which they have been receiving benefit and location of appropriate offices at which they are required to attend (as identified in the Schedule) (“relevant persons”).

items.title: The Insolvent Partnerships (Amendment) Order 1996
The Fossil Fuel Levy (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fossil Fuel Levy Regulations 1990 (“the Principal Regulations”) (S.I. 1990/266).

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) Regulations 1995
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”) with the Regulations amending them as listed in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Tables of Rates of Annuities) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the basis on which the Government Actuary is to prepare tables of rates of annuities for the purposes of Chapter IV of Part XIV of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (personal pension schemes).

items.title: The Income Tax (Employments) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/744) (“the principal Regulations”). In addition to an amendment of a drafting nature, the amendments make a number of changes, including the revocation of two regulations, in connection with the operation of PAYE following the introduction of self assessment for the year 1996/97 and subsequent years of assessment. Other Regulations amending the principal Regulations in connection with self assessment are S.I. 1995/1284 and 1996/804.

items.title: The National Health Service (Appointment of Consultants) (Wales) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations provide for the procedure to be followed by Health Authorities and Special Health Authorities in Wales when appointing medical and dental practitioners to consultant posts.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (Subsidy) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (Subsidy) Regulations 1994, subject to the saving provisions in regulation 4, to provide for the particulars which are required to be provided by an authority to the Secretary of State on the making of claims for housing benefit subsidy and council tax benefit subsidy.

items.title: The Severn Bridges Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Severn Bridge Regulations 1993. (“the 1993 Regulations”) which did not apply to the new Severn Bridge. These Regulations apply to the existing and new Severn Bridges and their toll plaza areas. The Regulations provide for the following matters:—

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Oil and Chemical Pollution of Fish and Plants) (Partial Revocation No. 2) Order 1996
The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1996
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Uncertificated Securities) (Extension of Scope of Act) Order 1996
items.description: (This is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Capital Allowances Act 1990, section 33A, (Appointed Day) Order 1996
The Education (Schools) Act 1992 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1996
The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) Order 1996
items.description: This Order specifies an agreement and a description of agreements to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1979 Act”) is deemed not to apply and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100(1) of the Electricity Act 1989 electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that the agreement specified in Article 2(2) of the Order and agreements meeting the description set out in Article 2(4) thereof nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) Regulations 1994. Most of the amendments are consequential to amendments made by the Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1331) to the Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/648, amended by S.I. 1995/838). There are also minor and drafting amendments.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination (Sub-leases and Sub-tenancies) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations modify and supplement the provisions of section 16 of and Schedule 4 to the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 in relation to cases where premises are occupied under a sub-lease or sub-tenancy. Section 16 and paragraph 1 of Schedule 4 are modified so that “lessor” refers to the occupier’s immediate landlord. The effect of this is that any consent to alterations has to be sought from the immediate landlord rather than a superior landlord.

items.title: The Education (Grant-Maintained and Grant-Maintained Special Schools) (Finance) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Statutory Maternity Pay (General) Amendment Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Statutory Maternity Pay (General) Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement No.2) Order 1996
The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variations) Order 1996
The Deregulation (Long Pull) Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Marylebone Road/Park Crescent Mews West, Westminster) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1996
The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment) Order 1996
The Fertilisers (Sampling and Analysis) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout Great Britain, consolidate and supersede the Fertilisers (Sampling and Analysis) Regulations 1991 (SI. 1991/973), the Fertilisers (Sampling and Analysis) (Amendment) Regulations 1991 (SI. 1991/2824) and the Fertilisers (Sampling and Analysis) (Amendment) Regulations 1994 (SI. 1994/129). They implement the Directives listed in paragraph 2 below.

items.title: The A501 Trunk Road (Camden and Islington) Red Route (Bus Lanes) Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The A501 Trunk Road (Camden) Red Route (Bus Lane) (No. 1.) Experimental Traffic Order 1996
The Social Security and Child Support (Jobseeker’s Allowance) (Consequential Amendments) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the regulations listed below. The amendments are consequential on the coming into force of the Jobseekers Act 1995, which replaces income support for the unemployed and unemployment benefit with jobseeker’s allowance. The Regulations replace references to unemployment benefit with references to jobseeker’s allowance, and add references to jobseeker’s allowance where the existing regulations refer to income support.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Regulations 1988 which provide for the remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the National Health Service Act 1977 and for the payment of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital. Regulation 2 amends the financial disregards applied in calculating student income.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Alternative Method of Calculation of Gain or Loss) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Exchange Gains and Losses (Alternative Method of Calculation of Gain or Loss) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/3227) (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision for an alternative method of calculation to be used in certain circumstances for finding the amount to be treated as an exchange gain or loss under the provisions in Chapter II of Part II of the Finance Act 1993.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Deferral of Gains and Losses) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
The Exchange Gains and Losses (Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations amend the Exchange Gains and Losses (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 199413226) (“the principal Regulations”). Regulations 3,5 to 7, and 9 make amendments which reflect the provisions of the Finance Act 1996 for taxing and relieving profits or losses on loan relationships with effect from 1st April 1996. Regulations 4 and 8 amend provisions of the principal Regulations for the making of elections.

items.title: The Radioactive Material (Road Transport) (Great Britain) Regulations 1996
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2 of the Radioactive Material (Road Transport) Act 1991. They replace the Radioactive Substances (Carriage by Road) (Great Britain) Regulations 1974 and the Radioactive Substances (Carriage by Road) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1985 (“the previous regulations”).

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Compensation (Amendment) Order 1996
The Brucellosis and Tuberculosis (England and Wales) Compensation (Amendment) Order 1996
The Recreational Craft Regulations 1996 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 94/25/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions of the member States relating to recreational craft (the Directive). Subject to certain limited exceptions, references in the Regulations to the Community or a member State include respectively a reference to the EEA or an EEA State, which are defined in regulation 2(2)(a).

items.title: The Gas Act 1986 (Exemptions) (No. 3) Order 1996
items.description: Section 6A of the Gas Act 1986, substituted by section 4 of the Gas Act 1995, provides for the granting by Order of exemptions from the prohibition contained in section 5 of the 1986 Act from carrying on the conveyance, supply or shipping of gas without a licence. The Order contains an exemption in respect of the conveyance of gas from the premises mentioned in the Schedule to a pipe-line system operated by a public gas transporter.
